model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, increasing batch size lets the model see substantially more training examples within the same verifier budget. A modest learning-rate increase keeps optimization progress per step from becoming too conservative after the batch-size increase, while preserving the rest of the script unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The cosine schedule over 256 steps regressed, so this candidate removes that change and tests decoupled weight decay instead. AdamW is a low-complexity optimizer-family change that often improves short-budget CNN training stability and generalization while keeping the successful larger-batch setup intact.","secondary_modes":["summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed step budget, increasing batch size further lets the model cover essentially the full training set within 256 updates. A small learning-rate increase keeps the optimizer from becoming too conservative after the batch-size increase, while leaving the successful AdamW setup otherwise unchanged.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current fixed-step setup is already covering most or all of the dataset, so additional representational capacity is a reasonable next lever. Increasing the base channel width is a minimal architecture change that should strengthen feature extraction while keeping the training loop and optimizer unchanged.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"After increasing convolutional width, the next low-risk capacity adjustment is a slightly larger hidden classifier. This keeps the training recipe unchanged while giving the wider feature extractor a bit more head capacity to translate improved features into lower validation loss.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The widened network and larger classifier improved substantially under the current recipe, which suggests the model may still benefit from slightly more aggressive optimization within the fixed 256-step budget. A modest learning-rate increase is the simplest next test and keeps the rest of the successful configuration intact.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current wider network and larger head are working well, so the next meaningful architecture step is one more convolutional stage. Increasing depth adds another round of feature extraction and downsampling while still keeping the model compact enough for the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The deeper three-stage network produced the largest improvement so far, which suggests the model is still benefiting from additional capacity. Increasing the base width is the cleanest follow-up and should strengthen the new deeper feature stack without changing the training recipe.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model has gained a lot of capacity and runtime is already climbing, so a low-cost regularization adjustment is a better next move than adding more layers or width. A moderately stronger AdamW weight decay may improve validation loss by tightening generalization without changing step budget or per-step compute.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"The current model is training in a short fixed-step regime where AdamW's default second-moment timescale may be slower than ideal. Reducing beta2 to 0.99 is a simple optimizer-dynamics change that can make adaptation more responsive without changing model capacity or compute.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.99)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The lower beta2 regressed, so this candidate restores the better AdamW moment setting and then tests a slightly more aggressive learning rate on top of the strongest architecture so far. This keeps compute unchanged while probing whether the current model is still under-optimized within the fixed step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The higher learning rate was slightly worse, so this candidate restores the better step size and then adds a small amount of dropout to regularize the now-deeper and wider network. This is a cheap robustness change that may improve validation loss without increasing compute further.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.0","old":"DROPOUT_RATE = 0.1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 80","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Dropout was clearly harmful in this short-budget setting, so this candidate removes it and spends the capacity budget on a slightly larger classifier instead. The network is already benefiting from added depth and width, and a modestly stronger head may extract a bit more value from those features without changing compute nearly as much as another convolutional expansion.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 80","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The larger classifier head regressed, so this candidate restores the stronger architecture and tests a lighter form of regularization at the loss level instead. Small label smoothing is cheap, keeps compute unchanged, and can improve validation cross-entropy on CIFAR-10 without the large optimization penalty that dropout introduced here.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Label smoothing did not beat the best plain-cross-entropy run, so this candidate restores the cleaner loss and spends the remaining search budget on one more width increase. Previous gains have come primarily from added capacity, and widening the already successful three-stage network is the most direct remaining architecture lever.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 20","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The extra width regressed and increased runtime, so this candidate restores the stronger 20-channel backbone and then tests a slightly smaller batch to reintroduce some gradient noise. With the model now much larger than the early baseline, 256 may be overly smooth for this fixed-step budget, and 192 is a conservative way to probe that tradeoff.","secondary_modes":["layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 2e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 3e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The smaller batch moved in the wrong direction, so this candidate restores the better 256-step geometry and then probes a milder amount of AdamW regularization. The jump from 1e-4 to 3e-4 only helped marginally, so 2e-4 is a reasonable interpolation that may preserve the benefit without over-regularizing the stronger model.","secondary_modes":["micro","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The best run so far came from a mild reduction in weight decay, which can shift the optimal step size slightly downward. A small learning-rate decrease is the cheapest remaining refinement and is safer than larger architecture changes this late in the search.","secondary_modes":["indexing","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.05e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last learning-rate reduction produced a small but real improvement, which suggests the optimum may be slightly lower still. With one step left, the highest-probability move is a final fine-grained downward adjustment rather than a larger architectural or optimizer change.","secondary_modes":["caching","indexing"]}
